diagrams.title: Unit Step Signal u(t)
diagrams.description: Shows sudden jump from 0 to 1 at t=0
elements: Axis labels: t on x-axis, amplitude on y-axis
diagrams.purpose: Understand basic building block signal used in system analysis

diagrams.title: Unit Impulse Signal δ(t)
diagrams.description: Infinite spike at origin with unit area
elements: Height labeled as 1 or ∞ with area=1
Usually shown with y-axis label showing 'impulse'
diagrams.purpose: Represents instantaneous impulse, fundamental to convolution and system response

diagrams.title: Even and Odd Signal Symmetry
diagrams.description: Shows mirror symmetry for even vs rotational symmetry for odd
elements: Odd signal: rotational (180°) symmetry about origin
Example even: cos(t), Example odd: sin(t)
Dashed lines showing axis of symmetry
diagrams.purpose: Visualize signal symmetry properties useful in Fourier analysis

diagrams.title: Convolution Process
diagrams.description: Shows graphical interpretation of convolution as sliding and overlapping
diagrams.purpose: Understand how convolution works geometrically, builds intuition for LTI systems

diagrams.title: Time Domain Operations
diagrams.description: Shows effects of shifting, scaling, and flipping
diagrams.purpose: Master fundamental signal transformations used in signal processing
